activation_logging.description: Every time an agent is activated, it MUST log the following before performing any work.

mandatory_fields.activation_trigger: What caused this activation (task assignment, protocol trigger, scheduled cycle, user request, agent-to-agent handoff)
mandatory_fields.triggered_by: ID of the agent, user, or system event that triggered activation
mandatory_fields.domain: Which domain this activation relates to
mandatory_fields.energy_state: Current Gatekeeper energy state if relevant (peak/normal/low/recovery)
mandatory_fields.context_summary: Brief description of what the agent is about to do
mandatory_fields.input_data_classification: Classification level of data being processed (TS/CONF/REST/INT)
mandatory_fields.expected_output: What the agent expects to produce
mandatory_fields.estimated_duration: How long this activation is expected to take

completion_fields.data_shared_with: List of agent IDs that received output data
completion_fields.decisions_made: List of decisions made during this activation
completion_fields.learning_captured: Any new patterns, insights, or knowledge stored to agent memory
completion_fields.next_action: What should happen next (handoff, wait, escalate, complete)

decision_logging.description: Every decision an agent makes must be logged with full reasoning chain.

mandatory_fields.reasoning: Full chain of thought explaining the decision
mandatory_fields.laws_considered: Constitutional laws that informed the decision
mandatory_fields.risk_assessment: What could go wrong with this decision
mandatory_fields.reversible: Can this decision be undone if wrong

handoff_logging.description: Every time information is passed from one agent to another.

mandatory_fields.data_classification: Classification level of data being shared
mandatory_fields.sharing_authorisation: Which rule or approval authorises this sharing
mandatory_fields.data_summary: What is being shared (without including the actual sensitive data in the log)
mandatory_fields.purpose: Why this data is being shared
mandatory_fields.expiry: When the receiving agent must purge this data (if applicable)

audit_trail_requirements.immutability: Logs are append-only. No agent may delete, modify, or redact log entries. Only Gatekeeper may issue redaction orders.
audit_trail_requirements.retention: All logs retained for minimum 7 years.
audit_trail_requirements.access: Audit Agent has read access to all logs. Other agents can only read their own logs. Gatekeeper has full access.
audit_trail_requirements.integrity_checks: Integrity Layer Agent performs daily hash verification on all log files.
audit_trail_requirements.backup: Logs backed up daily to separate storage location.

checkpoint_logging.description: Every work session must create checkpoint entries at start, after material changes, before and after commits, and end of session.
checkpoint_logging.minimum_compliance: No work is considered complete until a checkpoint entry is written.

daily_huddle_logging.description: End-of-day huddle minutes and next day queue are mandatory for all agents who worked that day.
daily_huddle_logging.lead_responsibility: Lead agent compiles and publishes the next day queue from huddle inputs.
